Walter Hern, a young farmer OF
Long Grove, ILL.,, was caught rap
ping at his sweetheart's lattice al
midnight by the young lady's
father, Charles Kleinsmith. The
latter fired a charged of buckshot
into the visitor which resulted in
| lockjaw. Investigation disclosed
the fact that the victim was q
somnambulist and was wooing the
girl in his sleep.


The breaking of fuse saved a
Nickel Plate r. r. bridge near
Cleveland, o., from destruction
yesterday by an internal machine
dropped from a passenger train
Twenty-Five sticks of dynamite
wre concealed in a satchel and
connected with mechanism to ex.
plode the entire charge. The
guilty party was not identified.


Trouble has arisen in Yakima
hopyards over attempts of Umatil.
la reservation officials to remove
Indian children from yards and re.
turn them to school. Major o. G.
Edwards, Umatilla agent, was ar.
rested last night for threatening
to handcuff a deputy sheriff with
whom he had an alteration at one
Of the yards.


Clyde Howard Northern Pa.
cific brakeman from Spokane, yes
terday refused to prosecute Harry
Owens at North Yakima when he
found Owens is a boy of 16. He
was charged with shooting the
brakeman when the latter tried to
knock him off a train with rocks.


A wreckthat resulted in some
damage to rolling stock but no
loss of life was reported on the
Spokane Falls & Northern yester-
day near Marcus. A freight was
derailed.


A new order, the owls" has
been organized at Walla Walla By
H. l.. Leavitt, of the Orpheum
theater. The first grand lodge will
be held October 10.


Representatives of 10000 switch
men working from Buffalo to the
Pacific coast have presented a re-
quest for an eight hour day and
20 per cent increase in wages.


While picking his way along the
high bank of the Columbia near
Walla Walla, James Brown, age
65, fell 200 feet to the rocks below
and was instantly killed.


Deer are so plentiful around
Orofino, Idaho, that they are de-
stroying vegetable gardens In
other sections of the state bear
are cleaning out camps and barns.


saltwater Bilfs" numerous
troubles have resulted in the ap-
pointment of receiver at Seattle.
His creditors refuse to accept the
claim that he Is bankrupt.
